title: Grande Bleu Jeju Yacht: Honest Sunset Cruise Review
content:
Published: 2025 · Last updated: 2025 · By Grande Bleu Yacht Crew · Verified Local Operator

Grande Bleu is Korea's only certified catamaran in Jeju, offering 60-minute sunset cruises from Daepo Port in Seogwipo.

If you've been scrolling through "things to do in Jeju" lists and everything looks the same, this one actually is different — and not for the reasons the brochures suggest.

Quick Facts

  • Vessel: Twin-hull catamaran (Korea's only certified brand)
  • Duration: 60 minutes
  • Departs: Daepo Port, Seogwipo, Jeju (GPS 33.2287°N 126.4318°E)
  • Capacity: Up to 91 guests across two boats
  • Price: ₩40,000–₩70,000 (child/adult)
  • Included: Unlimited wine, draft beer, tangerine juice, snacks, ramyeon, fishing gear
  • Best season: September–November (autumn golden hour)
  • Track record: 100,000+ guests served since 2011

Why a Catamaran Actually Matters

Most Jeju boat tours run single-hull vessels. They roll. Jeju's average coastal swell is only around 37cm, which sounds gentle until you've been on a mono-hull for forty minutes holding a glass of wine you can't actually drink.

A catamaran absorbs roughly 90% of that lateral motion through its twin hulls. In practice: you can stand on the deck, pour a glass, and not spill it. That's the entire pitch.

For a couple in their fifties — or anyone who's previously written off boat trips because of motion sickness — this is the structural reason Grande Bleu gets repeat bookings rather than one-and-done tourists.

It's also why the operator holds Korea's only certified catamaran brand status and partners with the Shilla Hotel and Parnas Jeju for guest excursions. Hotels at that tier don't recommend tours that make their guests queasy.

FAQ

How much does the Jeju yacht cost? The sunset tour is ₩70,000 for adults and ₩40,000 for children. The standard luxury tour is ₩60,000 for adults. Both include unlimited wine, beer, snacks, and the fishing experience — no hidden add-ons.

How long is the Jeju sunset cruise? Sixty minutes from departure to return. The route covers Wolpyeong Basalt Columns, Elephant Rock, sea caves, and Jingotnae Canyon, with a mid-cruise fishing stop included.

Is food included on the Jeju yacht? Yes — unlimited and free. Draft beer, red and white wine (served by the bottle), tangerine juice, water, local snacks, and hot ramyeon cooked onboard. There are no upgrade tiers or extra charges.

Where does the Jeju yacht depart from? Daepo Port in Seogwipo, on Jeju Island's south coast (GPS 33.2287°N 126.4318°E). It's about a 15-minute drive from central Seogwipo and 60 minutes from Jeju City.

Is the Jeju catamaran safe for kids and seniors? Yes. The twin-hull catamaran absorbs roughly 90% of wave motion, so seasickness is rare. A licensed captain and engineer are onboard, and Grande Bleu holds Jeju's highest safety certification.

What's the best time for sunset cruise in Jeju? Autumn — September through November. Cooler air, clearer skies, and sunset between 5:30 and 6:15pm align perfectly with the 5pm departure for full golden-hour coverage.
